profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review with explicit governance and limitation tracking.
notes: Allows compatibility harnesses and draft-aware governance snapshots.
Downgrades to review_required when submission gating or active errata require human judgment.

profiles.intendedUse: Pre-submission screening for whether a dossier is structurally eligible for regulator-facing packaging.
notes: Requires pinned governance snapshots and required disclaimers.
Current Dietary MCP model families are expected to fail this profile in Phase 1.

profiles.intendedUse: Consultation-oriented or exploratory review that tolerates draft-aware governance with explicit warnings.
notes: Allows consultation-oriented sources and compatibility harnesses.
Requires governance snapshots and consultation disclaimers to remain explicit.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review for microplastics and nanoplastics with explicit evidence and method-maturity limits.
notes: This profile preserves the emerging-contaminant family boundary and keeps pesticide engines out of scope.
Intended for review-oriented assessment support only.

profiles.intendedUse: Exploratory or consultation-oriented review of microplastics evidence with explicit maturity warnings.
notes: Allows emerging-evidence review with explicit not_allowed submission posture.

profiles.intendedUse: Hard-fail placeholder profile for submission-oriented microplastics use until reviewed official methods mature.
notes: Submission-oriented use is expected to fail by default in v0.1.
This profile exists to make the hard governance limit machine-readable rather than implicit.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review of PFAS food-contaminant evidence with explicit legal and monitoring provenance.
notes: This profile supports review-oriented PFAS source lookup and documentation without implying a native PFAS submission engine.

profiles.intendedUse: Exploratory or consultation-oriented PFAS review with explicit monitoring and evidence-context notes.
notes: Allows governed PFAS source and monitoring context to be surfaced for consultation-oriented review.

profiles.intendedUse: Hard-fail placeholder profile for submission-oriented PFAS use until a reviewed PFAS method pack is added.
notes: PFAS legal and scientific sources are represented in Dietary MCP, but no native PFAS submission-capable engine is published in v0.1.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review of acrylamide food-contaminant evidence with explicit mitigation and monitoring provenance.
notes: This profile supports review-oriented acrylamide source lookup and documentation without implying a native acrylamide submission engine.

profiles.intendedUse: Exploratory or consultation-oriented acrylamide review with explicit mitigation and occurrence-context notes.
notes: Allows governed acrylamide source and monitoring context to be surfaced for consultation-oriented review.

profiles.intendedUse: Submission-oriented acrylamide review with governed EFSA reference values, mitigation measures, and EU legal provenance.
notes: Governed scientific and legal sources are represented in Dietary MCP.
Submission-candidate evaluation proceeds through standard readiness rules when dossier governance and fingerprint requirements are met.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review of bisphenol dietary-exposure evidence with explicit food-contact and legal provenance.
notes: This profile supports review-oriented bisphenol source lookup and documentation without implying a native food-contact migration engine.

profiles.intendedUse: Exploratory or consultation-oriented bisphenol review with explicit food-contact migration context notes.
notes: Allows governed bisphenol source and legal context to be surfaced for consultation-oriented review.

profiles.intendedUse: Submission-oriented bisphenol review with governed EFSA TDI, food-contact migration limits, and EU legal provenance.
notes: Governed scientific and legal sources are represented in Dietary MCP.
Submission-candidate evaluation proceeds through standard readiness rules when dossier governance and fingerprint requirements are met.

notes: Governed scientific and legal sources are represented in Dietary MCP.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review against US EPA tolerance frameworks and reference values.
notes: Supports draft-aware governance for early US-bound dietary reviews.
Explicitly preserves EPA vs JMPR/EFSA reference value divergence.

profiles.intendedUse: Pre-submission screening against EPA 40 CFR Part 180 tolerance rules.
notes: Requires pinned US governance snapshots.
Will fail dynamically if cross-jurisdiction values (e.g. EFSA ADI) leak into the US assessment context.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review against Chinese GB standard frameworks and reference values.
notes: Supports governance for early China-bound dietary reviews.
Explicitly preserves GB vs EPA/EFSA reference value divergence.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review against Japanese Food Sanitation Act standard frameworks and reference values.
notes: Supports governance for early Japan-bound dietary reviews.

profiles.intendedUse: Internal scientific review against Canadian PMRA standard frameworks and reference values.
notes: Supports governance for early Canada-bound dietary reviews.
